subcodes.title: Words to George Winterburn et al, spoken on 1904-02-08
subcodes.first_line: All things were created for man, and man for God. Man is distinguished from all other creations. This is a special problem. There are three general classes of creations
subcodes.subjects: @man's distinction from the animal, @two aspects of the human soul; the higher and lower natures, @spiritual transformation, @degrees of spirit and the kingdoms of existence, @man is the sum; pinnacle; fruit of creation, @the mind's power of discovery, @second birth
